description: Rules for ensuring the application handles errors gracefully at every layer (UI, API, Forms).
philosophy: Errors are not exceptions; they are a predictable part of the user flow and must be handled elegantly.

_ai_agent_instructions.CRITICAL: Every generated component or hook that fetches data MUST include error handling.
_ai_agent_instructions.error_handling_pattern: Components should handle three states: loading, error, and success. Never render without checking isError state.
_ai_agent_instructions.error_messages: Always display user-friendly error messages, never raw API error details to users.

rules.name: Global UI Error Boundary
rules.description: The entire application MUST be wrapped in a React Error Boundary component.
rules.purpose: Catch unexpected rendering errors and display fallback UI instead of blank screen

rules.name: API Error Handling Mandate
rules.description: All data-fetching hooks MUST handle the `isError` state.
guidelines: If `isError` is true, render a specific error message or fallback component
Do not let API errors bubble up and crash the UI
Hook should return structured error object
Display user-friendly messages, not technical details

rules.name: Form Validation & Feedback
rules.description: All forms MUST provide clear, field-level validation feedback.
rules.implementation: Use Zod for schema validation + react-hook-form for error display at field level
